replies.msg:
65. Here, as we have already noted, the relevant context is the conferment of financial benefits on spouses in the contexts of employment and taxation. Traditionally, those benefits were not conferred in order to protect the institution of marriage or even to encourage people to marry one other. Instead, they were provided to acknowledge the economic reality of the family unit with one member of a couple, usually the male, being the principle breadwinner for the family and, in the case of employment within the civil service, to encourage the recruitment and retention of staff. Medical and dental benefits were therefore extended to a civil servant’s spouse and dependent children as a perquisite of employment. Joint tax assessment helped to lessen the overall tax burden on a couple living together and meeting expenses traditionally from one source of earned income. It was (and is) no part of the Secretary’s or Commissioner’s functions that they were responsible for protecting (much less promoting) the institution of marriage. The Secretary’s principal responsibility was (and is) the efficient administration of government and that of the Commissioner was (and is) the raising of revenue through the taxation system.

65. Here, as we have already noted, the relevant context is the conferment of financial benefits on spouses in the contexts of employment and taxation. Traditionally, those benefits were not conferred in order to protect the institution of marriage or even to encourage people to marry one other. Instead, they were provided to acknowledge the economic reality of the family unit with one member of a couple, usually the male, being the principle breadwinner for the family and, in the case of employment within the civil service, to encourage the recruitment and retention of staff. Medical and dental benefits were therefore extended to a civil servant’s spouse and dependent children as a perquisite of employment. Joint tax assessment helped to lessen the overall tax burden on a couple living together and meeting expenses traditionally from one source of earned income. It was (and is) no part of the Secretary’s or Commissioner’s functions that they were responsible for protecting (much less promoting) the institution of marriage. The Secretary’s principal responsibility was (and is) the efficient administration of government and that of the Commissioner was (and is) the raising of revenue through the taxation system.

終審法院在判詞指，梁鎮罡與其同性伴侶的同性婚姻是有效的，兩人的婚姻與異性婚姻同樣是公開及只有單一伴侶，終院認同香港法例所界定的婚姻制度，不過以欠缺共識為由而拒絕小眾人士的申索在原則上有損基本人權，道德觀念並非考慮因素。

終院認為，拒絕向梁鎮罡提供僱傭及稅務福利與保障香港的婚姻制度沒有合理關聯，法庭難以想像同性配偶被剝奪福利會驅使任何人締結異性婚姻，而且以異性婚姻是香港法律唯一承認的婚姻形式作理據，只讓異性已婚伴侶獨享福利，拒絕同性伴侶獲平等對待，這個循環論證存在邏輯謬誤。

終院認為，本案與同性伴侶是否有權根據香港法律締結婚姻沒有關聯。

終院又認為，公務員事務局要遵守平等機會僱用政策，而稅務條例亦認可多配偶婚姻制度，反映條例的目的亦非推廣香港法律所界定的婚姻制度，因此局方沒有充分理據，支持異性及同性已婚伴侶在公務員配偶福利及報稅的差別對待，裁定梁鎮罡上訴得直。

總結：唔係認同同性婚姻係香港合法而無法獲取已婚公務員褔利同合併報稅構成歧視 而係政府一方理據不足

但如該婚姻有潛在可能並且實際上是多配偶制婚姻，則婚姻一詞不包括一名男子與其正妻以外的其他妻子之間的婚姻；而結婚 (married)一詞須據此解釋； (由1989年第43號第2條增補)

(b)在香港以外任何地方由兩個有行為能力結婚的人按照當地法律而締結的婚姻，不論該婚姻是否獲香港法律承認，
但如該婚姻有潛在可能並且實際上是多配偶制婚姻，則婚姻一詞不包括一名男子與其正妻以外的其他妻子之間的婚姻；而結婚 (married)一詞須據此解釋； (由1989年第43號第2條增補)

所以政府用稅務條例做主要理據嚟打係嚴重不足

65. Here, as we have already noted, the relevant context is the conferment of financial benefits on spouses in the contexts of employment and taxation. Traditionally, those benefits were not conferred in order to protect the institution of marriage or even to encourage people to marry one other. Instead, they were provided to acknowledge the economic reality of the family unit with one member of a couple, usually the male, being the principle breadwinner for the family and, in the case of employment within the civil service, to encourage the recruitment and retention of staff. Medical and dental benefits were therefore extended to a civil servant’s spouse and dependent children as a perquisite of employment. Joint tax assessment helped to lessen the overall tax burden on a couple living together and meeting expenses traditionally from one source of earned income. It was (and is) no part of the Secretary’s or Commissioner’s functions that they were responsible for protecting (much less promoting) the institution of marriage. The Secretary’s principal responsibility was (and is) the efficient administration of government and that of the Commissioner was (and is) the raising of revenue through the taxation system.
